commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ae8d42644febf9bb0f51c6ac9a34e950c2e3a3ef)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a generated-media direct-delivery fallback when an active requester wake has failed and the requester-agent completion handoff throws, plus one regression test for a session write-lock timeout.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +56. Total +62 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/test level: the PR test drives active wake failure followed by a `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` from the requester-agent handoff. I did not see after-fix real behavior proof from a live or qa-channel run for the same failure shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer or contributor should narrow the message-delivery fallback and add real behavior proof before merge; missing contributor proof makes this a human review path rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix: fallback when generated media handoff locks
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the existing #85489 active-wake generated-media fallback intact.
- Add the missing narrow path for generated-media completions where the active requester wake already failed and the requester-agent handoff throws before returning delivery evidence.
- Reuse the existing `generated-media-direct` fallback and idempotency key so successful generated media is delivered instead of being converted into a failed completion notice.

## Why this is narrow

This does not change the normal active-session success path, dormant requester behavior, or generic requester-agent handoff failures. The fallback is only attempted when all of these are true:

- the completion is for an agent-mediated generated-media tool,
- expected media URLs are present,
- the active requester wake has already failed,
- the requester-agent handoff throws before producing a response,
- the existing direct media delivery target is available.

This specifically covers the observed Spartacus failure shape: generation succeeded, active wake failed with `source_reply_delivery_mode_mismatch`, the requester-agent handoff then hit a session write lock, and the generated image was reported as failed instead of being delivered.

## Real behavior proof

Regression added in `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`:

`directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks`

The test exercises the real `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` path with controlled boundaries:

- active requester wake returns `transcript_commit_wait_unsupported`, then `no_active_run`, matching the active-wake failure path,
- requester-agent handoff throws `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError: session file locked (timeout 60000ms): pid=43`, matching the observed lock failure class,
- generated image completion includes `/tmp/generated-locked.png`,
- fallback sends exactly that generated media URL through `sendMessage`,
- idempotency key is `announce-channel-media-handoff-locked:generated-media-direct`.

Observed passing output:

```text
✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks
✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  150 passed (150)
```

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --reporter=verbose` -> passed, `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 150 passed (150)`.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.

## Known baseline issue

- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm tsgo:core` currently fails on unchanged upstream/main code:

```text
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.abort-settle-timeout.ts(4,3): error TS2322: Type 'ProcessEnv' is not assignable to type 'Pick<ProcessEnv, "OPENCLAW_EMBEDDED_ABORT_SETTLE_TIMEOUT_MS" | "OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST">'.
```

That file is untouched by this PR; the PR diff is limited to `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` and `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
